'''Mark Applegarth''' (born 10 December 1984), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Mash"''', is an English professional [[rugby league]] coach who is the head coach of the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]], and a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played during the 2000s and 2010s. He played at club level for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], the [[York City Knights]] (two spells), the [[Central Queensland Capras|Central Queensland Comets]] and the [[Batley Bulldogs]], as a {{rlp|pr|sr}}, or {{rlp|lf}}.<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

He took over as head-coach of [[York Knights|York]] in the [[RFL Championship]], following [[Andrew Henderson (rugby league)|Andrew Henderson]]'s move to become Head of Rugby operations and development.

==Background==
Applegarth was born in Newton Hill, [[Wakefield]], [[West Yorkshire]], England. As of 2020, he lives in the Wakefield area with his wife Jessica, they were married at Sandburn Hall, [[Flaxton, North Yorkshire|Flaxton]], [[York]] during 2018.

==Playing career==
He represented the Great Britain Schoolboys against the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]] during 2002.{{cn|date=October 2022}} Applegarth came through the ranks at Wakefield Trinity Wildcats and made his début as an 18-year-old. Upon leaving Wakefield Trinity Wildcats due to injuries, he joined the York City Knights. He was released from his contract with the York City Knights during June 2010 to pursue a career in Australia. After a brief stint with the Temora Dragons in New South Wales, Applegarth played for the [[Central Queensland Comets]] based in [[Rockhampton]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] competition. During 2012, Applegarth signed for the  [[Batley Bulldogs]] under his former Wakefield Trinity Wildcats coach [[John Kear]]. During 2014, after another serious knee-injury, Applegarth re-signed for the York City Knights to become their player/assistant coach. He retired from playing during 2016 and as of 2019, he was the Head of Youth at [[Wakefield Trinity]].{{cn|date=October 2022}}

==Coaching career==
===Wakefield Trinity===
Applegarth was appointed head coach following the sacking of [[Willie Poching]] at the end of the [[Super League XXVII|2022 Super League]] season. In round 15 of the 2023 Super League season, Applegarth earned his first win as Wakefield Trinity head coach defeating [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] 24-14. Wakefield had lost their opening 14 matches of the campaign.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65822026|title=Super League: Wakefield 24-14 Leeds - Bottom club chalk up first win|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
At the conclusion of the [[Super League XXVIII]] season Wakefield Trinity finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight. Applegarth was subsequently relieved of his duties and replaced with [[Daryl Powell]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
===York Knights===
On 5 June 2024, it was reported that he would take over the head coach role from [[Andrew Henderson (rugby league)|Andrew Henderson]] at [[York Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=TotalRL|date=5 Jun 2024|title=Ex-Wakefield Trinity boss Mark Applegarth appointed to Championship head coach role|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-wakefield-trinity-boss-mark-applegarth-appointed-to-championship-head-coach-role/}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, Applegarth guided York to their 1895 Cup final victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]]. In the 2025 RFL season, Applegarth guided York to first place on the table and eventual grand final where they would lose narrowly to [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cm26dp2l3vvo |title=York pip Featherstone in extra time to win 1895 Cup |work=BBC Sport |date=7 June 2025 |access-date=8 June 2025}}</ref>
